transliteration:
tad-buddhayas tad-ātmānas tan-niṣṭhās tat-parāyaṇāḥ
gacchanty apunar-āvṛttiṁ jñāna-nirdhūta-kalmaṣāḥ
literal_meaning: Those whose intellect, self, steadfastness and refuge are in That (the Supreme), whose stains are washed away by knowledge, attain non-returning.
contextual_meaning: When every layer of personality—thoughts, identity, loyalty and ultimate shelter—aligns with a single transcendent reference point, the psyche becomes self-cleansing and moves irreversibly toward wholeness.
psychological_state: Unified consciousness; ego boundaries soften into an overarching identity anchored beyond the personality, producing spontaneous freedom from rumination and compulsive action.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing professional realizes that achievements never quiet the inner critic; after a silent retreat she experiences a non-dual glimpse and re-orients life around service rather than success, ending burnout.
anchor_text: Center every part of yourself in the Supreme and knowledge will wash away all stains forever.
